sections.header: 2. Programme Details — Duration, Seats, Eligibility, Vision and Mission
sections.text: Contains the key programme details for B.Tech ECE-ACT including duration, seat count, eligibility criteria, and the department's vision and mission statements.

sections.header: B.Tech ECE Advanced Communication Technology — Programme Details
sections.text:
Duration: 4 years (Regular) / 3 years (Lateral Entry)

No. of Semesters: 8 (Regular) / 6 (Lateral Entry)

Intake / No. of Seats: Total — 30 (Government — 15, Management — 15)

Eligibility: 10+2 system of Education. Must have secured a pass in Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics in the qualifying examination.

Curriculum & Syllabus (2021 Regulation): <https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/academics/2021Regulation.pdf>

Scope for Higher Studies: M.E. / M.Tech. / M.B.A.

#### Vision:

To be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient Communication Engineers through sustainable practices.

#### Mission:

M1. Impart core fundamental knowledge and necessary skills in communication systems through innovative teaching and learning methodology.

M2. Inculcate critical thinking, ethics, lifelong learning and creativity needed for industry and society.

M3. Cultivate the students with all-round competencies, for career, higher education and self-employability.

Note: NRI Quota applies — 5% of sanctioned seats (approximately 1–2 seats) are reserved under NRI quota as per standard Anna University / TNEA norms.

sections.header: 6. Employment by Role, Government Initiatives and Skills/Certifications Demand
sections.text: The following data is from the Employment Opportunities by Role (2025–2030) — Advanced Communication Technology table: Telecom Engineer (5G/6G) has high employment opportunities with skills in RF design, Antenna design, mmWave, LTE, 5G NR, and network optimization. Satellite Communication Engineer has high employment opportunities with skills in Satellite design, payload integration, frequency planning, and communication systems. IoT Network Engineer has very high employment opportunities with skills in Low-power protocols (LoRa, NB-IoT), sensor networks, and embedded systems. Wireless Communication Specialist has moderate to high employment opportunities with skills in Wi-Fi 6, mmWave, optical fiber, SDR, and broadband deployment. Research Engineer (6G/Quantum Comms) has high employment opportunities with skills in Quantum computing, 6G technologies, and advanced wireless systems. RF System Engineer has high employment opportunities with skills in Signal processing, RF components, and microwave engineering. Network Security Engineer has very high employment opportunities with skills in Network protocols, cybersecurity, 5G security, and encryption.

1. National Digital Communications Policy (NDCP 2018): Aimed at positioning India as a global leader in communication technologies and attracting investment in 5G and beyond.
2. Space Policy: Promoting private-sector participation in satellite communications.
3. Smart Cities Mission: Generating demand for IoT, broadband, and wireless communication infrastructure.
4. PLI Scheme (Production Linked Incentive): Boosting manufacturing of telecom equipment and network components in India, leading to job creation.

sections.header: Skills and Certifications Demand in Industry:
sections.text:
1. 5G & 6G Expertise: Certifications from 3GPP, IEEE, and Cisco.
2. IoT & Embedded Systems: Courses on Arduino, Raspberry Pi, STM32, and LoRaWAN.
3. Satellite Communications: Hands-on experience with Satcom equipment and ground stations.
4. Wireless Protocols: Knowledge of Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth 5.0/5.1, LoRa, and NB-IoT.
5. Software-Defined Networking (SDN): Familiarity with SDN and Network Function Virtualization (NFV).

The following data is from the Role table: Telecom Engineer (5G/6G) has High job growth requiring skills such as RF design, Antenna design, mmWave, LTE, 5G NR, and network optimization. Satellite Communication Engineer has High job growth requiring skills such as Satellite design, payload integration, frequency planning, and communication systems. IoT Network Engineer has Very High job growth requiring skills such as Low-power protocols (LoRa, NB-IoT), sensor networks, and embedded systems. Wireless Communication Specialist has Moderate to High job growth requiring skills such as Wi-Fi 6, mmWave communication, optical fiber, SDR, and broadband deployment. Research Engineer (6G/Quantum) has High job growth requiring skills such as Quantum computing, 6G technologies, and advanced wireless systems. RF System Engineer has High job growth requiring skills such as Signal processing, RF components, and microwave engineering.

The following data is from the Role table: Network Security Engineer has Very High job growth requiring skills such as Network protocols, cybersecurity, 5G security, and encryption.

This chunk answers what skills are needed to become a Network Security Engineer after ECE-ACT, what certifications should be obtained after B.Tech ECE-ACT at MSJCE, what the demand is for IoT Network Engineers in India, how the PLI scheme creates jobs for ECE graduates, what NDCP 2018 is and how it affects ECE job opportunities, what skills are needed for a 5G/6G Telecom Engineer, which ECE roles have very high job growth by 2030, and what certifications are in demand for ECE-ACT graduates.

Keywords include Telecom Engineer, Satellite Communication Engineer, IoT Network Engineer, Network Security Engineer, RF System Engineer, Wireless Communication Specialist, Research Engineer, 5G NR, LTE, antenna design, 3GPP, IEEE, Cisco, Arduino, Raspberry Pi, STM32, LoRaWAN, Bluetooth 5.0, SDN, NFV, NDCP 2018, PLI scheme, Smart Cities Mission, and Space Policy.

Sector-wise growth from 2025–2030 includes IoT & Embedded Systems at 15–18% per year which is the highest, Satellite Communications at 12–15% per year, R&D & Next-Gen Comms at 10–14% per year, Telecom (5G/6G) at 10–12% per year, and Broadband & Wireless at 8–10% per year.
